title: Optimize the Dynamic Trie Node Inserter

statement.markdown: Given a dynamic trie data structure, implement a function to insert a new word into the trie. The function should return the updated trie. Note that the word can contain duplicate characters and the function should handle this case correctly. The trie is represented as a tree where each node has a value and a map of child nodes.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the word to be inserted into the trie.
statement.notes: The function should handle words of varying lengths and should return the updated trie in O(n) time complexity, where n is the length of the word.

examples.explanation: Insert the word 'apple' into the trie
Insert the word 'banana' into the trie

hints.text: Use a recursive approach to insert the word into the trie

editorial.title: Optimal Solution
editorial.intuition: The key idea is to use a recursive approach to insert the word into the trie.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves iterating over each character in the word and inserting it into the trie. However, this approach has a time complexity of O(n^2) due to the nested loops.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach involves using a recursive function to insert the word into the trie.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a recursive function to insert the word into the trie. The base case is when the word is empty, in which case the function returns the current node. Otherwise, the function recursively calls itself with the remaining characters in the word and the updated node.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution is proven by induction on the length of the word.
editorial.dryRun: Let's consider an example where the word is 'apple'. We start at the root node and recursively insert each character in the word.
commonMistakes: Not handling the base case correctly
